content.problemStatement:
Write a program to find the sum of all the numbers in the array that are multiples of 3.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test contain two lines of inputs:

The first line contains a positive integer NNN - the length of the array.
Second line of each test case contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of the array.




Output Format

For each test case, print on the new line : sum of the 333's multiples present in the array
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers denoting the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the sum of all array elements that are multiples of 3.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: multiples of 3 are 3 and 9, sum = 12. Second test case has no multiples of 3, sum = 0.
The only element 3 is a multiple of 3, so the sum is 3.

solution.approach: For each test case we iterate through the N numbers, check if a number is divisible by 3 using the modulo operator (x % 3 == 0). If it is, we add it to a running total stored in a 64-bit integer to avoid overflow. After processing all numbers we output the total. The algorithm is linear in the size of the input.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage
